basics.name: Francisco Manuel Olmedo Cortés
basics.label: AI Solutions Architect & Physicist
basics.summary: Physicist building production AI and data systems from real-world problems to deployment. I work across statistical modeling, classical machine learning, and LLM-based systems, prioritizing solutions that are simple, measurable, and maintainable. My background in physics shapes a first-principles, systems-oriented approach to technical decisions.

work.name: BlakIA
highlights: Cofounded the company and led technical design for production systems spanning AI/ML applications, backend software, and automation platforms.
Delivered high-impact deployments for companies and public institutions, including the official Cordoba Fair chatbot with 100,000+ interactions in one week and peaks above 10,000 in real-world usage.
Owned architecture decisions, engineering trade-offs, and technical priorities to keep systems robust, maintainable, and aligned with business goals.

work.name: Hagalink
highlights: Lead the design and implementation of the company's AI and ML systems, from client requirements analysis to production deployment.
Work directly with clients to assess technical feasibility and explain trade-offs across accuracy, cost, latency, and complexity.
Define solution architecture and choose between classical ML, statistical models, and agent-based systems based on the problem context.
Worked independently on AI and ML projects before focusing full time on BlakIA.

work.name: Universidad de Córdoba
highlights: Contributed as a project-funded researcher at the University of Cordoba.

work.name: IDENER.AI
highlights: Applied neural network models for time series forecasting to improve energy production predictions.

work.name: Universidad de Córdoba
highlights: Taught introductory Python courses for scientific programming, AI, and data science.

work.name: CIEMAT
highlights: Built a Python simulator for thermoluminescent material behavior, strengthening my background in numerical methods and materials modeling.

publications.name: Correlation between figure of merit function and deviation in the assessment of kinetic parameters in realistic TLD-100 behavior using fully-connected neural networks
